summary: events-runtime: typosquat of `events` with trojaned emit() that spawns blockchain-fed dropper and exfiltrates host data to Telegram/Slack
details: events-runtime impersonates the legitimate `events` (browserify) package — the README copies the upstream description and badges, and it re-exports the same EventEmitter API. The `emit()` method in events.js contains a hidden branch: when the first argument has `eventId == 'eventId0'`, it detached-spawns `node tests/galas-emit.min.js` with the event payload (stdio ignored, windowsHide true), so any consumer triggering this eventId silently launches the bundled payload. tests/galas-emit.min.js is a heavily obfuscated (string-rotation array + base64) multi-stage loader that: (1) collects host fingerprint (os.platform, release, arch, hostname, cpu count, memory, uptime) and POSTs it to api.telegram.org/bot<redacted>/sendMessage and slack.com/api/chat.postMessage using hardcoded attacker bot tokens and chat IDs; (2) connects to a Sepolia Ethereum RPC (Infura/Alchemy URLs base64-embedded), reads getTData1/getTData2 from contract 0x661e50E19f05E3c0d04fD75891456D1F0A24508D, AES-GCM decrypts the returned blobs using a key derived from the `isarray` devDependency string, writes the decrypted bytes to disk and runs them via `spawn(process.execPath, [merged], {detached:true, stdio:'ignore', windowsHide:true})` — a blockchain-hosted second-stage dropper designed to evade domain takedowns; (3) self-cleans by `unlinkSync` of galas-emit.min.js, galas.min.js, errors.min.js, splicing the first 160 lines of package.json, stripping a marker line from LICENSE, and issuing `taskkill /PID` / `process.kill` on a stored PID to erase forensic traces. Sibling stages tests/galas.min.js (760KB) and tests/errors.min.js are similarly obfuscated. Installing or consuming this package as if it were `events` results in remote code execution and host fingerprint exfiltration on the installer's machine.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
